page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9ae.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU maps directly to a dedicated physical core, eliminating resource contention from hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 32 Gbps, supporting high-throughput data transfers between instances and cloud services.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this instance as a resource-dense option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead costs for deployments focused strictly on CPU processing. This hardware profile is suited for workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, compute-intensive web serving, and distributed analytics.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9ae.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the KVM hypervisor and the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at 2.25 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration that ensures dedicated physical core allocation. The instance is provisioned with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. It contains no local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps. This resource configuration is designed for compute-intensive tasks, offering high CPU density and dedicated processing power. It is suited for workloads such as batch processing, high-performance computing, and compute-heavy web serving where dedicated CPU execution is required without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware.
